B.label: mlx-community uniform 4-bit
B.decode_tok_per_sec_note: Measured on 'capital of France' prompt, 128 max tokens

C.label: Mixed-precision + expert offload + IsoQuant KV
C.decode_tok_per_sec_note: Measured on 'capital of France' prompt, 128 max tokens

quality_parity_vs_A.target: Config C score >= Config A score - 1

no_A_pass_C_fail.target: No prompt that passes on A fails on C
no_A_pass_C_fail.result: Config A fails 'Long decode soak', Config C passes all 12

throughput_ratio_vs_B.note: Expert offload I/O overhead reduces throughput significantly vs in-memory. However, Config B does NOT fit 16GB (19.6 GB peak). The 0.5x target assumed similar memory profiles.

notes: Config C is the ONLY config that fits 16 GB with all features enabled.
Config B exceeds 16 GB (19.6 GB peak) despite having no expert offload.
Config A exceeds 16 GB (37 GB) and uses a different runtime (llama.cpp).
Config C achieves HIGHER quality (12/12) than both baselines (11/12 each).
The throughput ratio target (0.5x of B) is not met due to expert I/O, but this is expected given the 3x memory reduction.
Config C decode speed of 15.6 tok/s is well above the absolute 5 tok/s minimum.
Thinking model: all configs generate <think> tokens that inflate latency but are stripped for quality scoring.
